title: caylusea abyssinica
intro: Caylusea abyssinica, also known as the Abyssinian or Khat, is a flowering plant native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula, belonging to the Capparaceae family. Traditionally, Caylusea abyssinica has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to increase alertness and energy due to its high concentration of alkaloids, primarily cathinone. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fatigue, improving cognitive function, and treating conditions like asthma, anemia, and digestive issues. In traditional medicine, Caylusea abyssinica can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, infusions, or chewing the leaves, although its recreational use is also widespread.
